title: Execution Framework
subtitle: Agent Execution Framework Design
description: The Agent execution framework is the core infrastructure of AI Agent engineering, responsible for coordinating the Agent lifecycle, task scheduling, step execution, and result delivery. A well-designed execution framework ensures Agents complete complex tasks efficiently and reliably while supporting asynchronous processing, error recovery, and performance optimization.
sub_problems: Execution engine architecture: How to design a general-purpose execution engine supporting synchronous/asynchronous and serial/parallel execution
Step orchestration and flow control: How to manage multi-step execution flows, conditional branching, and loop control for Agents
Timeout and resource limits: How to implement execution timeout detection, memory/CPU constraints, and resource isolation
Execution state tracking: How to maintain execution context, intermediate results, and complete execution traces
Performance and throughput: How to optimize batch processing, concurrent execution, and resource utilization
best_practices: Adopt event-driven architecture to decouple execution flows into composable event handlers
Implement hierarchical timeout management mechanisms, distinguishing global timeouts, step timeouts, and operation timeouts
Use state machine patterns to standardize Agent execution phases (initialization → execution → completion → cleanup)
Provide structured execution logging and tracing capabilities for debugging and performance analysis
Design extensible execution strategy interfaces supporting custom scheduling, retry, and fallback logic
